items.body:
## Documentation Gap **Package:** `@azure-typespec/http-client-csharp-provisioning` (generator) **Service directory:** `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-provisioning/` **Triggered by:** b524f0a13a0572efbb7ae548f2bce5b86bc61713 (PR #58482) by `@ArcturusZhang` ## What Changed PR #58482 introduced two significant behavioral changes to the provisioning generator: 1. **Reachability-based model/enum filtering** ‚Äî `ProvisioningOutputLibrary` now walks resource models' property graphs recursively (`CollectReachableTypes()`) and only passes reachable `InputModelType`/`InputEnumType` instances to the type factory. This replaces the old approach of emitting all input models/enums and relying on the post-processor to prune unreferenced types. 2. **Shared resource model handling** ‚Äî `ProvisioningTypeFactory.CreateModelCore` now handles the case where one `InputModelType` backs multiple `ProvisioningResourceProvider` instances. It selects the canonical provider whose `ResourceMetadata.ResourceName` matches the model's own name (alphabetically first as a tiebreaker), ensuring flattened properties are forwarded correctly. ## Gaps Found - **`design.md` ¬ß 6 "Output Library"** currently says: *"Only resources are marked as 'types to keep' ‚Äî the post-processor automatically prunes unreferenced models and enums."* This is no longer accurate. The filtering now happens up-front via `CollectReachableTypes()` in `ProvisioningOutputLibrary`, not via post-processor pruning. The section must be updated to describe the reachability walk (base/derived models, properties, array/dictionary/union element types) and explain why it is needed (avoid dead types like `XxxListResult` paging envelopes, patch wrappers, and error models). - **`design.md` ¬ß 5 "TypeFactory & Type Resolution"** (or ¬ß 6 "Resource Provider") has no mention of the shared resource model scenario where a single `InputModelType` backs more than one `ProvisioningResourceProvider`. The canonical provider selection logic and why it is necessary (so `FlattenPropertyVisitor` builds `OutputFlattenPropertyMap` on the correct parent resource) is undocumented.
üìê Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above ### Step 1: Modify files **File:** `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-provisioning/docs/design.md` **Action:** Edit **Change 1 ‚Äî Replace the "Output Library" paragraph (¬ß 6)** Locate this text: ```` The output library bypasses the mgmt output pipeline (which would trigger mgmt-specific type initialization) and instead iterates input models and enums directly, routing each through our TypeFactory. Only resources are marked as "types to keep" ‚Äî the post-processor automatically prunes unreferenced models and enums. ``` Replace with: ``` The output library bypasses the mgmt output pipeline (which would trigger mgmt-specific type initialization) and instead builds the set of models and enums to emit explicitly via a **reachability walk** (`CollectReachableTypes`). Starting from every resource model registered in `ArmProviderSchema.Resources`, it recursively traverses base/derived types, all property types, and element types of array, dictionary, and union types. Only models and enums reachable through this walk are passed to the type factory. This avoids emitting dead types that have no place in a provisioning library ‚Äî for example, paging envelope types (`XxxListResult`), patch/update wrappers, and generic error models. These types appear in the full input model graph but are never referenced from a resource's property surface. ```` **Change 2 ‚Äî Add a "Shared Resource Models" subsection** In ¬ß 5 "TypeFactory & Type Resolution", after the existing "Resource Detection" subsection, add: ```markdown ### Shared Resource Models In rare cases the same `InputModelType` backs more than one `ProvisioningResourceProvider` (for example, when `Azure.ResourceManager.Legacy.LegacyOperations` creates a virtual sub-resource whose payload reuses the parent resource's model). `CreateModelCore` must return a single `ModelProvider` for each input model. When the output library records multiple providers for one model, the type factory selects the **canonical provider** ‚Äî the one whose `ResourceMetadata.ResourceName` matches the model's own name. If no exact match exists, it falls back to the alphabetically first resource name. This ensures the `FlattenPropertyVisitor` attaches flattened forwarding properties to the parent resource (e.g., `Profile` carries the flattened `Description` / `SkuName`), while the sub-resource (e.g., `ProfileRevision`) only exposes the properties it needs. ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets No README snippets are required for this change ‚Äî the affected file is an internal design document, not a user-facing README. ### Step 3: Verify README structure The file being updated (`docs/design.md`) is an internal design document, not a user-facing package README. Standard README section validation does not apply. ### Step 4: Validate This is a documentation-only change (Markdown). No build or test steps are required. After editing: 1. Confirm the Markdown renders correctly (no broken headings, fenced blocks, or list formatting). 2. Optionally run a Markdown linter if one is configured for the repo.
## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above
items.title: [Provisioning Generator] Docs: Update design.md for reachability-based model filtering and shared resource model handling
